activities.description: Book Club meets Friday mornings in Hall A for readers who like a good story and a better conversation. Each week the group digs into the season’s pick—fiction or nonfiction—trading impressions, favorite passages, and the occasional plot twist that nobody saw coming. Whether you finish every page or listen along, you are welcome: the table is round, the tone is neighborly, and the coffee is imaginary until someone brings a thermos. Season on the calendar: January 8 through March 26 each year. For winter 2027, Friday January 8 is the eighth day of the month (not the 10th)—that Friday is the first session—and the block runs through Friday March 26, 2027. The recurring sidebar lists Book Club only in that seasonal window; confirm the current title, any skipped weeks, and holiday breaks on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Book Club—Fridays 10:30 AM, Hall A (Jan 8–Mar 26). Discuss the season’s pick with neighbors—new readers welcome.

activities.description: Bible Study is a midweek pause in Hall A for neighbors who want to read scripture together, ask honest questions, and encourage one another in faith. Each Wednesday morning offers discussion, prayer, and fellowship—no sermon scorekeeping, just the Word opened in community. Bring your favorite Bible translation; newcomers are welcome whether you have studied for decades or are curious about your first structured look at a passage. Season on the calendar: January 13 through March 31 each year (e.g. the 2027 block from Wed Jan 13 through Wed Mar 31). The recurring schedule only lists Bible Study during that window, like our other seasonal activities; confirm topics or holiday breaks on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Bible Study—Wednesdays 10:00 AM, Hall A (Jan 13–Mar 31). Scripture, discussion, and fellowship; newcomers welcome.

activities.description: Evening worship service in Hall A—a time for prayer, music, and reflection together as a park community. Season on the calendar: January 4 through February 28 each year (e.g. Jan 4–Feb 28, 2027). Visibility in the recurring schedule follows that seasonal window; confirm times on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Vespers—Sundays 5:00 PM, Hall A (season Jan 4–Feb 28). Evening worship; check calendar.

activities.description: Come learn the basics of martial arts with 1st Degree Black Belt and park resident Jyo Kyo Nim John Barkle (495 Grapefruit). We will learn body conditioning, very basic self defense, and do a light low impact workout designed to help us stay mobile and continue to enjoy life.
activities.adCopy: Come learn the basics of martial arts with 1st Degree Black Belt and park resident Jyo Kyo Nim John Barkle (495 Grapefruit). We will learn body conditioning, very basic self defense, and do a light low impact workout designed to help us stay mobile and continue to enjoy life.

activities.description: Picture the energy of a knock-down, drag-out bar fight—then trade the chaos for chalk, sunshine, and discs sliding the way classic bar shuffleboard intended. Horsecollar is that level of competitive theater, only nobody's swinging anything heavier than good-natured trash talk. Bar-table rules, teams, multiple rounds, a cumulative score that won't cut anyone slack—Sundays feel like the drama got upgraded and the furniture stayed upright. When the tally locks, one crew walks off swaggering; the rest quietly vow round two.
activities.adCopy: Knock-down, drag-out bar-fight energy (shuffleboard edition): bar rules, teams, multi-round grudge tally, zero flying furniture. Bragging rights on the line. Sun 11:30 AM–2:30 PM, courts.

activities.description: Mexican Train Dominoes games
activities.adCopy: Mexican Train Dominoes games

activities.description: Card Bingo is a fast, easy-to-learn table game with simple rules, low buy-in, and cash pots. Each table has 4 players using 1 deck of cards. Players race to empty their hands as the dealer flips cards one at a time.
activities.adCopy: Card Bingo is a fast, easy-to-learn table game with simple rules, low buy-in, and cash pots. Each table has 4 players using 1 deck of cards. Players race to empty their hands as the dealer flips cards one at a time.

activities.description: Swedish weaving (often worked on monk’s cloth or huck fabric) is a decorative surface technique—think beautiful towels, runners, and gifts with rhythmic, easy-to-follow patterns. This weekly circle meets in Hall C while the program runs from early November through the end of March: neighbors share tips, troubleshoot stitches, and enjoy laid-back company. Beginners welcome; bring supplies if you have them or ask what to pick up. The schedule uses November 4 through March 31 each year (aligned with 2026–2027 dates). While Swedish weaving is in season, the broader Stitch & Bitch crafting circle takes a break and resumes in spring.
activities.adCopy: Swedish Weaving—Wednesdays 1–3 PM, Hall C (Nov 4–Mar 31). Decorative weaving on monk’s cloth/huck—patterns, towels, and gifts with neighbors. Beginners welcome.

activities.description: Exercise with Debbie
activities.adCopy: Exercise with Debbie

activities.description: You're invited: the Winter Ice Cream Social is a friendly pause for anyone in the park—no RSVP, no fee. It meets on the third Sunday of each month during the season; each month spotlights a flavor of the week at the scoop table: classic vanilla, tangy orange sherbet, frosty root-beer floats, or whatever surprise Sandy lines up next. Bring the kids, bring your sweet tooth, and meet neighbors you usually only wave at from the driveway. Thirty minutes of cold spoons, warm conversation, and small-town winter fun.
activities.adCopy: Free Winter Ice Cream Social—3rd Sunday each month, 1:00–1:30 PM, Hall B. Flavor of the week (vanilla, sherbet, floats & more). Kids welcome; all residents invited.

activities.description: Same scoop, warmer months: the Summer Ice Cream Social picks up when the Winter edition takes its break—third Sunday of each month March through mid-November. No fee, no RSVP; each date features a flavor of the week (vanilla, orange sherbet, root-beer floats, or Sandy’s pick). Kids and residents drop by Hall B for half an hour of cones, bowls, and conversation with neighbors.
activities.adCopy: Free Summer Ice Cream Social—3rd Sunday each month (Mar–Nov), 1:00–1:30 PM, Hall B. Flavor of the week. Kids welcome; all residents invited.

activities.description: Pull up a chair in Hall C and watch a block of wood become something worth showing off. Steve Heveron hosts Wood Carving with Steve—a relaxed, hands-on circle for small-scale projects with big personality: custom pens that feel like heirlooms, whimsical yard birds that make mailboxes smile, and other pocket-sized treasures. This isn’t timber-frame construction or all-weekend marathons—think fine detail, sharp tools, and neighborly coaching in a friendly hall. Curious first-timers welcome; old hands welcome too. Come sniff the shavings, trade tips, and go home with a little more swagger and maybe a project you brag about at the mail kiosk. Season: Tuesdays in Hall C, November 2 through March 30 (last Tuesday of March 2027 is March 30).
activities.adCopy: Wood Carving with Steve—Tuesdays 1–3 PM, Hall C (Nov 2–Mar 30). Custom pens, yard birds, and small carvings with Steve Heveron—sharp tools, great company, no big construction.

activities.description: Pétanque (say it with a little flair—pay-tonk) is the traditional French boules game that turns a patch of court into a coffeehouse: metal boules, a tiny target called the jack (cochonnet if you’re feeling continental), and neighbors taking turns rolling or tossing to cozy their balls closest—or politely evict someone else’s. If you know bocce ball, you’re already fluent: it’s the same happy genre (get near the pallino, guard your lie, celebrate a beautiful carom), just with Gallic swagger and a famously social pace. The point is points—whoever’s boules hug the jack after the dust settles—and the vibe is inclusive, outdoor, and dangerously easy to love. We gather Mondays and Thursdays while the court is in season: November 1 through March 29 at the pétanque court in the northwest corner of the park—bring shades when the sun’s sharp, bring banter always, maybe bring a friend you’ll beat by a whisper.
activities.adCopy: Pétanque Mon & Thu 9:15 AM, NW court (season Nov 1–Mar 29)—French boules with a bocce soul: cozy up to the jack, finesse a roll, knock a rival ball off cruise control. Beginners welcome.

activities.description: Stained Glass Class is where curiosity meets color: cut, foil, solder (or follow the steps your project needs), and watch daylight turn into something you made with your own two hands. It’s the joy of learning a new skill alongside neighbors—laughing over a tricky curve, celebrating a clean bead of solder, and walking away with panels, sun-catchers, or small pieces that look like they belong in a gallery but hang in your window instead. You don’t need a fine-art degree—just a willingness to try, a little patience, and a taste for glass that glows when the sun hits it. Mondays and Thursdays in Hall C through the cool months: come create something beautiful, one piece at a time.
activities.adCopy: Stained Glass Class—Mon & Thu 1:00–2:00 PM, Hall C (season Nov 2–Mar 29). Learn the craft while you build gorgeous glass projects: color, light, and neighbors cheering you on.

activities.description: Hand & Foot is the lively partnership card game that looks like canasta’s chatty cousin: big melds, lots of decks on the table, and every player working through two piles—the “hand” first, then the “foot.” You draw, you discard, you slap down clean melds when you can, and you curse (politely) when someone freezes the discard pile at the worst moment. It’s easy to pick up beside a friendly neighbor who will walk you through your first foot, and satisfying once the patterns click. Monday nights in Hall A during the cool season are for cards, coffee-level concentration, and laughter when someone goes out on a flourish. Beginners welcome; regulars always seem to have an extra seat.
activities.adCopy: Hand & Foot (cards)—Mondays 6:30–8:00 PM, Hall A (Nov 1–Mar 29). Partner meld game with hand and foot piles; neighbors teach newcomers. Easy to join, hard to quit.

activities.description: Computer Class is your weekly excuse to peek behind the screen—without jargon jail. We wander the world of PCs together: what’s new, what’s worth knowing, and how to stay comfortable with the tech that runs daily life. Each hour is part tour, part show-and-tell—fresh concepts, trustworthy basics, and the occasional “they moved the button again” survival tip. You’ll brush up on current software habits, safer browsing, useful shortcuts, and cloud ideas in plain English. Bonus round: you might finally tame that pocket computer you keep calling a phone—photos, messages, updates, and fewer accidental pocket-dials. No grades, no intimidation—just neighbors, coffee-table curiosity, and keyboards that feel a little less mysterious when you leave. Class meets Tuesdays in season from November 10 through March 3; check the main calendar for the precise weekly pattern outside those dates.
activities.adCopy: Computer Class—Tuesdays 9:30–10:30 AM, Hall C (season Nov 10–Mar 3). PCs, today's tech, and maybe that phone in your pocket—plain language, demos, Q&A.

activities.description: Bring your favorite crafting project to our weekly crafting circle. Enjoy good company, learn or teach a new skill, and spend time with neighbors—while Swedish weaving runs in Hall C from early November through March, this open crafting circle picks up the rest of the year. Examples include animal stuffing, loom knitting, crochet, needlepoint, and macrame. No skill or experience is needed.
activities.adCopy: Stitch & Bitch—Wednesdays 1–3 PM, Hall C (Apr 1–Nov 3). Open crafting circle—bring your project; neighbors swap tips on crochet, macrame, and more. Swedish weaving meets here in winter.

activities.description: The McAllen Mobile Home Park Association bylaws group meets on the first Wednesday of each month, starting right after the regular Wednesday association meeting adjourns. The association meeting begins at 9:00 AM; the Bylaws Committee session follows immediately—often within a few minutes, depending on how long the business meeting runs. This is a formal working session: members review current association business, discuss updates, and work through issues facing the park association. Attendance and conduct should reflect the seriousness of the committee's role—no joking around during the meeting. Season on the calendar: November 4 through March 3 each year (aligned with Nov 4, 2026–Mar 3, 2027). Confirm exact start time and room on the main calendar.
activities.adCopy: Bylaws Committee—1st Wed after 9:00 AM association meeting, Hall B (season Nov 4–Mar 3). Starts when business meeting ends; check calendar.

activities.description: Weekly meeting of the McAllen Mobile Home Park Association. Residents discuss current events, hear updates on upcoming activities, deconflict schedules, and work through park business together. This session is a chance for all park members to stay informed and be part of the solution. Summer season on the calendar: April 1 through November 3 each year (weekly Wednesdays through the day before the winter slot begins November 4).
activities.adCopy: MMHPA association meeting—Wednesdays 9–10 AM, Hall A (summer Apr 1–Nov 3). Discuss park news, activities, and deconflict; stay informed.

activities.description: Weekly meeting of the McAllen Mobile Home Park Association. Residents discuss current events, hear updates on upcoming activities, deconflict schedules, and work through park business together. This session is a chance for all park members to stay informed and be part of the solution. Winter season on the calendar: November 4 through March 31 each year (weekly Wednesdays; summer slot resumes April 1).
activities.adCopy: MMHPA association meeting—Wednesdays 9–10 AM, Hall B (winter Nov 4–Mar 31). Discuss park news, activities, and deconflict; stay informed.

activities.description: Sewing Circle is a relaxed weekly stitch-and-chat in Hall C: bring a project (repairs, quilting, or something new), share tips, and sew alongside neighbors. No competition—just thread, conversation, and the small triumph of a straight seam. Season runs from November 5 through the last Thursday in March each year; the calendar model uses November 5 through March 31 so late-March meetings still appear—confirm the final Thursday on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Sewing Circle—Thursdays 1–2 PM, Hall C (Nov 5–last Thu in Mar; calendar Nov 5–Mar 31). Bring your project; neighbors stitch together.

activities.description: Whist is a classic English trick-taking card game—think of it as bridge’s dignified ancestor. Four players in two fixed partnerships (partners sit across from each other) use a standard 52-card deck. In traditional whist the dealer’s last card is turned up to set the trump suit; players take turns leading tricks, following suit when they can and trying to win tricks for their side. The game was enormously popular through the 18th and 19th centuries and is still enjoyed at social “whist drives” and friendly tables. Our group meets Monday evenings in Hall A for a few weeks in late winter—drop in for a hand, watch a rubber, or ask a partner to walk you through the basics.
activities.adCopy: Whist (classic trick-taking cards)—Mondays 6:45–8:00 PM, Hall A (Jan 24–Feb 21). Four players, two teams; beginners welcome.

activities.description: Watercolor Painting is a bring-your-own-project morning in Hall C—no stiff still-life mandate, no pace race. Everyone works on something personal: maybe a loose landscape from last winter’s trip, a pot of porch geraniums, a wet-on-wet sky study, or exercises in glazing and lifting color you’ve been meaning to try. Watercolor rewards curiosity—transparent washes, happy accidents, and the slow build of a passage you can walk away from and fix next week. Neighbors share tips on paper, brushes, and mixing greens that don’t look like neon pickles, but the real magic is the quiet hour when brushes dip, drips land where they mean to, and someone says, “I like where that’s going.” Beginners can start with a simple leaf or sky strip; experienced painters keep chipping away at that tricky foreground. Season on the calendar: January 4 through March 29 each year (aligned with Mondays from early 2027 forward); confirm the opening Monday on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Watercolor Painting—Mondays 9:30–11:00 AM, Hall C (Jan 4–Mar 29). Personal projects in transparent watercolor; tips, demos, and neighbor encouragement.

activities.description: Crafting Workshop with Linda invites you into Hall C for a friendly, hands-on afternoon of making things together. Each week may highlight different arts and crafts—paper, fabric, seasonal décor, simple home projects, or whatever the group votes in—so you can try something new or bring a work-in-progress and find a seat at the table. There is no pressure to be “good at art”; the goal is good company, shared supplies, a few laughs over glue dots, and the small win of walking out with something you touched from start to finish. Beginners welcome; regulars can swap tips while Linda keeps the room organized and welcoming. Season on the calendar: January 18 through March 29 each year; the main park calendar confirms weekly topics, supply notes, and any schedule changes.
activities.adCopy: Crafting Workshop with Linda—Fridays 1:00–3:00 PM, Hall C (Jan 18–Mar 29). Come create with neighbors; projects vary week to week.

activities.description: Line Dancing in Hall B is your low-pressure on-ramp to country and pop choreography everyone thinks they’ll never get—then suddenly they’re grinning at the mirror row. Over the season you might step through favorites like the Electric Slide, Cupid Shuffle, Tush Push, Cowboy Boogie, or a simple waltz-line pattern, with plenty of repeats so muscle memory kicks in. You don’t need a partner: lines move together, calls come from the front, and the beat does half the teaching. Most folks pick up the basic pattern in one session—good enough to join the group confidently—and many love that they can learn in the morning and still recognize the steps if music shows up that same night at a social. Comfortable shoes, water, and a sense of humor are the only real prerequisites. Season on the calendar: January 5 through March 23 each year; confirm the weekly playlist and any room changes on the main park calendar.
activities.adCopy: Line Dancing—Mondays 10:00–11:00 AM, Hall B (Jan 5–Mar 23). Learn easy patterns in one session—Electric Slide, Cupid Shuffle, and more; dance that night with confidence.

activities.description: Garden Flag Painting is a bright, social workshop for neighbors who want a cheerful welcome at the curb without wrestling a sewing machine. On the fourth Tuesday of each month (during the late-winter window), we gather in Hall C to brush, stencil, or freehand paint garden flags—think seasonal blooms, patriotic stars, welcome wording, snowbirds, or a holiday motif you can swap when the breeze turns. Supplies and patterns vary by session; beginners get step-by-step help while experienced painters trade tricks for crisp edges and smooth base coats. You’ll walk out with a flag ready to slip on a standard garden stand and show off by your walk or site. Season on the calendar: January 26 through February 23 each year; only the fourth Tuesday in that span qualifies, so check the main park calendar for the exact January and February dates and any supply fee.
activities.adCopy: Garden Flag Painting—4th Tuesday 10:30 AM–12:00 PM, Hall C (Jan 26–Feb 23). Paint a welcome flag for your yard; themes and supplies vary—check calendar.

Join us this Memorial Day as we come together to honor the sacrifices of our Armed Forces and remember those who gave everything in service to our nation. Dinner begins at 12:00 PM and will feature classic hot dogs and authentic Iowa-style Maid-Rite sandwiches. This is a community pot luck, so guests are encouraged to bring their favorite sides and desserts to share. Enjoy good food, great company, and a moment of gratitude as we celebrate the spirit of remembrance and fellowship together.

features.eventName: Save Kitty Fundraiser DJ Dance — DJ Dana dance fundraiser
features.adCopy: Come dance, have fun, and help save Kitty, DJ Dana's pit bull terrier. Kitty had a tumor removed, and the surgery was expensive. There is no cover charge for this Hall B fundraiser, but donations will be accepted at the event to help pay for Kitty's surgery. Requests are welcome, so bring your favorite songs, enjoy each other's company, and help a neighbor save a beloved dog.
features.description: Come dance, have fun, and help save Kitty, DJ Dana's pit bull terrier. Kitty had a tumor removed, and the surgery was expensive. There is no cover charge for this Hall B fundraiser, but donations will be accepted at the event to help pay for Kitty's surgery. Requests are welcome, so bring your favorite songs, enjoy each other's company, and help a neighbor save a beloved dog.

features.eventName: Father's Day Potluck — Father's Day dinner
features.adCopy: Father's Day at McAllen Mobile Park belongs to the dads, granddads, stepdads, uncles, and every father-figure who shows up for this community year after year. Join us for a hearty potluck dinner with neighbors who know how to cook and how to tell a story—bring a favorite dish to share, pull up a chair, and pass the plates with pride. Please bring your own plates, silverware, and drinks. Stop by Hall A in the days ahead to sign up and give us a head count so we can welcome everyone comfortably. Dinner begins at 1:00 PM. When the table quiets down, wander over to Hall B for our monthly ice cream social at 1:45 PM—cones, bowls, and a sweet finish to the afternoon together.
features.description: Father's Day at McAllen Mobile Park belongs to the dads, granddads, stepdads, uncles, and every father-figure who shows up for this community year after year. Join us for a hearty potluck dinner with neighbors who know how to cook and how to tell a story—bring a favorite dish to share, pull up a chair, and pass the plates with pride. Please bring your own plates, silverware, and drinks. Stop by Hall A in the days ahead to sign up and give us a head count so we can welcome everyone comfortably. Dinner begins at 1:00 PM. When the table quiets down, wander over to Hall B for our monthly ice cream social at 1:45 PM—cones, bowls, and a sweet finish to the afternoon together.

features.adCopy: On this Sunday we scoop right after the Father's Day potluck wraps in Hall A. Same friendly third-Sunday tradition May through October: free ice cream in Hall B, flavor of the week, cones and bowls, 1:45–2:15 PM. Kids and residents welcome; no fee or RSVP.
features.description: On this Sunday we scoop right after the Father's Day potluck wraps in Hall A. Same friendly third-Sunday tradition May through October: free ice cream in Hall B, flavor of the week, cones and bowls, 1:45–2:15 PM. Kids and residents welcome; no fee or RSVP.

features.adCopy: Karaoke night is a familiar McAllen Mobile Park feature where neighbors can sing, listen, and spend an easy evening together in the hall. Check the date and time for the current scheduled night.

Celebrate the 4th of July with neighbors at a picnic and potluck in Hall B. Sandwiches will be provided as the main course by the Summer MMP Group. Please sign up in Hall A to bring a salad, side dish, other food, or dessert.
features.description: Celebrate the 4th of July with neighbors at a picnic and potluck in Hall B. Sandwiches will be provided as the main course by the Summer MMP Group. Please sign up in Hall A to bring a salad, side dish, other food, or dessert.

features.eventName: 4th of July Dance — DJ Dana Karaoke Party
features.adCopy: Come one, come all, and help us celebrate our nation's independence with fun family music, dancing, and song. DJ Dana will host the dance party, take music requests, and keep the evening moving with favorites residents can sing along to. Bring snacks, drinks, and your best holiday spirit. This can be a memorable 4th of July night, but only if our residents come out, fill the hall, make requests, dance, sing, laugh, and celebrate together.

features.adCopy: Come out and spend time with friends and enjoy music by DJ Kushman. This Benefit Dance supports McAllen Mobile Home Park resident Todd Baker, whose mother recently passed away after a long struggle. Todd incurred debt while caring for her and must travel back north to handle final arrangements. Our goal is to raise $2,000 to help with travel and final expenses.

features.adCopy: Share a relaxed Sunday lunch with neighbors at the August Luncheon Potluck in Hall B. Bring a favorite dish, try something homemade, and enjoy an easy afternoon around the table. Please sign up in Hall A so we know you are coming and note what you plan to bring. Main dishes, salads, vegetables, desserts, and other favorites are all welcome.

Seven nights aboard MSC Seascape from Galveston—Costa Maya, Roatan, and Cozumel. McAllen Mobile Park neighbors are exploring a group sailing with possible discounts and perks. See pricing, what's included, and how to join.
